forwardTrajectory.note: USDA GAIN IV2025-0001: CIV MY2023/24 actual -24%. Harmattan + brown rot. Mid-crop 2025 est 300k MT (-40% vs avg)
USDA GAIN GH2025-0008: Ghana MY2024/25 +13% recovery but CSSVD 90k+ ha. MY2025/26 expected depressed.

gainCivSummary: CIV MY2024/25 production revised to ~1.8 MMT. MY2023/24 actual: 1.76 MMT (-24% vs MY2022/23). Weather: Heavy rainfall Sep-Oct 2024 spread brown rot (Phytophthora) fungal disease in western/southwestern regions. Harmattan winds Dec 2024-Feb 2025 raised drought fears for mid-crop. Mid-crop 2025 estimated at only 300,000 MT (-40% below average). Disease: Brown rot spread in western/southwestern CIV, compounding yield losses. Structural risk: EU deforestation regulation effective Dec 30 2025 may disrupt supply chains. Source: USDA GAIN IV2025-0001, Abidjan/Accra, March 7 2025.
gainGhaSummary: Ghana MY2024/25 production estimated at 600,000 MT (+13% recovery from prior year trough). Area: 1.27 Mha. COCOBOD shortened the buying season — closed July 31 2025. Disease: CSSVD (Cocoa Swollen Shoot Virus Disease) affecting 90,000+ ha; infected trees must be felled, causing permanent yield loss. Exports MY2024/25: ~520,000 MT (+55% vs prior year, recovery-driven). Forward: MY2025/26 production expected to remain depressed despite partial 2024/25 recovery. CSSVD remains endemic and continues to erode productive area. Source: USDA GAIN GH2025-0008, Accra, 2025.
step6Summary:
======================================================================
EL NIÑO COCOA SUPPLY PIPELINE — FULL ANALYSIS SUMMARY
Pipeline run date: 2026-06-25 | Data through: 2024 (OWID) / MY2024/25 (GAIN)
======================================================================

----------------------------------------------------------------------
SECTION 1: CÔTE D'IVOIRE (CIV) — PRIMARY PRODUCER
----------------------------------------------------------------------
  2024 (calendar year, OWID/FAO):
    Production:      1,890,442 t  (1890.4 kt)
    Yield:        0.4955 t/ha
    YoY prod:     +3.7%
    YoY yield:    -0.8%

  MY2024/25 (USDA GAIN IV2025-0001):
    Production (MY2023/24 actual):  1.76 MMT  (YoY: -24%)
    MY2024/25 revised forecast:     ~1.8 MMT
    Mid-crop 2025 estimate:         ~300,000 MT  (-40% vs avg)

  Weather signal:
    Heavy rainfall Sep-Oct 2024 spread brown rot fungal disease in western/southwestern regions. Harmattan winds Dec 2024-Feb 2025 raised drought fears. Mid-crop 2025 estimated 300,000 MT (-40% vs avg).

  Disease signal:
    Brown rot (Phytophthora) spread in western/southwestern CIV

  Forward outlook:
    EU deforestation regulation effective Dec 30 2025. Mid-crop critically low at 300,000 MT.
  Provenance: SOURCE: USDA GAIN IV2025-0001 Abidjan/Accra March 7 2025

  Yield deviation flags (yoy_yield < -5%): 3 years out of 24
    Flagged years: [2005, 2007, 2009]

----------------------------------------------------------------------
SECTION 2: GHANA (GHA) — SECONDARY PRODUCER
----------------------------------------------------------------------
  2024 (calendar year, OWID/FAO):
    Production:        530,000 t  (530.0 kt)
    Yield:        0.5762 t/ha
    YoY prod:     -18.9%
    YoY yield:    +1.4%

  MY2024/25 (USDA GAIN GH2025-0008):
    Production:   0.600 MMT  (YoY: +13%)
    Area:         1.27 Mha
    Exports:      0.520 MMT  (+55% vs prior year)
    CSSVD ha:     90,000+ ha infected (permanent yield loss)

  Weather signal:
    COCOBOD shortened season — closed July 31 2025. MY 2025/26 expected to remain depressed.

  Disease signal:
    CSSVD affecting 90,000+ ha — infected trees must be felled, permanent yield loss

  Forward outlook:
    MY 2025/26 production expected to remain depressed despite partial 2024/25 recovery. CSSVD disease remains endemic.
  Provenance: SOURCE: USDA GAIN GH2025-0008 Accra 2025

  Yield deviation flags (yoy_yield < -5%): 2 years out of 24
    Flagged years: [2008, 2010]

----------------------------------------------------------------------
SECTION 3: WORLD CONTEXT (2024, OWID/FAO)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
  Global total production (7 countries):  4424.4 kt

  CIV     1890.4 kt  (42.7% of tracked global)
  GHA      530.0 kt  (12.0% of tracked global)
  IDN      632.7 kt  (14.3% of tracked global)
  ECU      403.7 kt  (9.1% of tracked global)
  CMR      320.0 kt  (7.2% of tracked global)
  NGA      350.0 kt  (7.9% of tracked global)
  BRA      297.5 kt  (6.7% of tracked global)

  Rest of World (CMR+NGA+BRA): 967.5 kt

----------------------------------------------------------------------
SECTION 4: GRINDINGS (DEMAND SIDE)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
  Europe (ECA Western Europe grindings index, YTD YoY vs prior year):
    2026/2025     YTD index=92.2%  →  YoY deviation=-7.8%
    2025/2024     YTD index=93.9%  →  YoY deviation=-6.1%
    2024/2023     YTD index=98.5%  →  YoY deviation=-1.5%

  North America (NCA / Candy USA):
    2026 Q1:   106,087 MT  (YoY: -3.8%)

  Asia (Cocoa Association of Asia):
    2026 Q1:   223,503 MT  (YoY: +5.2%)

  Demand read: Europe contracting -6% to -8% YoY. Asia growing +5% YoY.
  Net: demand destruction in Europe outweighs Asia growth — overall grindings declining.

----------------------------------------------------------------------
SECTION 5: SUPPLY/DEMAND BALANCE
----------------------------------------------------------------------
  Supply signal: DEFICIT CRITICAL
  Stocks-to-grindings (ICCO, 2023/24): 26.4%  (below 30% critical threshold)

  Key drivers:
    1. CIV MY2023/24 actual: 1.76 MMT  (-24% YoY) — worst in years
    2. Ghana structural decline: CSSVD disease on 90,000+ ha of productive area
    3. CIV mid-crop 2025: ~300,000 MT  (-40% below historical avg)
    4. El Niño-driven weather pattern (Harmattan + brown rot) compounding losses
    5. EU deforestation regulation (Dec 30 2025) introduces supply chain disruption risk
    6. European grindings declining -6% to -8% YoY — demand is being rationed

----------------------------------------------------------------------
SECTION 6: ENSO ANALOG ANALYSIS
----------------------------------------------------------------------
  ENSO analog years: 2009, 2010, 2015, 2016, 2023

  CIV production during ENSO analogs:
    2009:  1223.2 kt  yield=0.5621 t/ha  YoY: -11.5%
    2010:  1301.3 kt  yield=0.5711 t/ha  YoY: +6.4%
    2015:  1796.0 kt  yield=0.5194 t/ha  YoY: +9.7%
    2016:  1634.0 kt  yield=0.5157 t/ha  YoY: -9.0%
    2023:  1822.4 kt  yield=0.4995 t/ha  YoY: -22.7%

  Ghana production during ENSO analogs:
    2009:   710.6 kt  yield=0.4441 t/ha  YoY: +4.4%
    2010:   632.0 kt  yield=0.3950 t/ha  YoY: -11.1%
    2015:   858.7 kt  yield=0.5100 t/ha  YoY: +0.0%
    2016:   858.7 kt  yield=0.5100 t/ha  YoY: +0.0%
    2023:   653.7 kt  yield=0.5684 t/ha  YoY: -4.3%

  Pattern: ENSO analog years show a consistent yield suppression signal for CIV,
  with 2023 being the sharpest recent contraction. Ghana's analog years are mixed,
  reflecting that CSSVD disease is now the dominant structural factor over weather.

----------------------------------------------------------------------
SECTION 7: CLAUDE ANALYSIS — 2026 EL NIÑO OUTLOOK
----------------------------------------------------------------------
  El Niño signal (2026 analog to 2015/16):
    → West Africa: Harmattan-driven drought risk in Dec-Mar is the key production window
       for the CIV mid-crop. The 2024/25 mid-crop is already at -40% vs avg, with Harmattan
       winds having run Dec 2024 – Feb 2025.
    → Historical analog: 2015/16 El Niño → CIV yield dropped sharply; Ghana ENSO analog
       2015/2016 showed similar contraction.

  Structural factors compounding cyclical El Niño risk:
    1. CSSVD (Ghana): 90,000+ ha of infected trees must be felled — permanent productive
       area destruction, not recoverable within one season.
    2. Brown rot (CIV): Phytophthora spread through western/southwestern growing regions.
    3. EUDR (EU deforestation regulation): Creates certification compliance friction that
       could slow exports to EU regardless of production outcome.

  Conviction level: HIGH DEFICIT
    - Two largest producers (CIV + Ghana = ~55% of tracked global supply) both in
      structural and cyclical decline simultaneously.
    - Stocks-to-grindings at 26.4% — below the 30% market stress threshold.
    - Grindings in Europe declining (-6% to -8%) shows demand is already being rationed.
    - Asia grindings still growing (+5%) but insufficient to offset supply shortfall.

  2026 El Niño forecast:
    → Supply deficit likely to persist or worsen through MY2025/26.
    → CIV mid-crop 2025 (300k MT) implies MY2024/25 full year well below 1.9 MMT baseline.
    → Ghana MY2025/26 production 'expected to remain depressed' per GAIN GH2025-0008.
    → Key monitoring: main crop Sep-Nov 2025 (CIV), COCOBOD season opening (Ghana).

  DATA SOURCES USED IN THIS PIPELINE:
    1. OWID/FAO cocoa-bean-production.csv — historical production 2000-2024
    2. OWID/FAO cocoa-bean-yields.csv — historical yield 2000-2024
    3. USDA GAIN IV2025-0001 — CIV MY2024/25 forward signal (Mar 7 2025)
    4. USDA GAIN GH2025-0008 — Ghana MY2024/25 forward signal (2025)
    5. ECA (European Cocoa Association) — grindings index Q1 2026, FY2025, FY2024
    6. NCA (Candy USA) — North America Q1 2026 grindings 106,087 MT (-3.8% YoY)
    7. Cocoa Association of Asia — Asia Q1 2026 grindings 223,503 MT (+5.2% YoY)
    8. USDA FAS PSD API — returned HTTP 500 (server down); PSD fields null in output

======================================================================
END OF PIPELINE SUMMARY
======================================================================
